subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: Determine whether the given values can serve as the values of a distribution function of a random variable with the range $x = 1, 2, 3, 4$. $F(1) = 0.5$, $F(2) = 0.4$, $F(3) = 0.7$, and $F(4) = 1.0$
explanation: Since $F(2) < F(1)$, $F$ is not increasing and therefore $F$ can not be a cdf. Proposition 25.3 $F$ is continuous from the right. That is, $\lim_{t\to b^+} F(t) = F(b)$. Proof. Let $\{b_n\}$ be a decreasing sequence that converges to $b$ with $b_n\ge b$ for all $n$. Define $E_n = \{s : X(s)\le b_n\}$. Then $\{E_n\}_{n\ge 1}$ is a decreasing sequence of events such that $\bigcap_{n=1}^\infty E_n = \{s : X(s)\le b\}$. By Proposition 25.1 we have $\lim_{n\to\infty} F(b_n) = \lim_{n\to\infty} \Pr(E_n) = \Pr\left(\bigcap_{n=1}^\infty E_n\right) = \Pr(X\le b) = F(b)$. Proposition 25.4 (a) $\lim_{b\to-\infty} F(b) = 0$ (b) $\lim_{b\to\infty} F(b) = 1$. Proof. (a) Note that $\lim_{x\to-\infty} F(x) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F(x_n)$ where $(x_n)$ is a decreasing sequence such that $x_n\to-\infty$. Define $E_n = \{s\in S : X(s)\le x_n\}$. Then we have the nested chain $E_1\supseteq E_2\supseteq E_3\supseteq\cdots$. Moreover, $\emptyset = \bigcap_{n=1}^\infty E_n$. By Proposition 25.1, we find $\lim_{x\to-\infty} F(x) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F(x_n) = \lim_{n\to\infty} \Pr(E_n) = \Pr\left(\bigcap_{n=1}^\infty E_n\right) = \Pr(\emptyset) = 0$. (b) Note that $\lim_{x\to\infty} F(x) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F(x_n)$ where $(x_n)$ is an increasing sequence such that $x_n\to\infty$. Define $E_n = \{s\in S : X(s)\le x_n\}$. Then we have the nested chain $E_1\subseteq E_2\subseteq E_3\subseteq\cdots$. Moreover, $S = \bigcup_{n=1}^\infty E_n$. By Proposition 25.1, we find $\lim_{x\to\infty} F(x) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F(x_n) = \lim_{n\to\infty} \Pr(E_n) = \Pr\left(\bigcup_{n=1}^\infty E_n\right) = \Pr(S) = 1$
